title: Fear Garden — Chapter 5: The Root Ledger
description: Chapter 5 of the Fear Garden saga. The final case reveals how the earlier deaths, fraud, fake vendors, footage sales, and staged logins were all roots of the same scheme.

answerKey.method: Used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame Trevor
answerKey.motive: To cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the Beer Garden expansion
answerKey.keyEvidence: Duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving trevor’s login was staged
answerKey.explanation: Lena Vale had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving Trevor’s login was staged with the failed statement: Lena said she only handled guest check-in. The duplicate badge and shell lease ledger connect every earlier chapter into one final answer.
answerKey.sagaConnection: The duplicate badge and shell lease ledger connect every earlier chapter into one final answer.
answerKey.chapterRevealHook: Lena Vale did not commit every crime in the garden, but she built the system that made every crime useful. Fear Garden closes with the Root Ledger exposed—and one outside name still blacked out.

publicClues.title: Chapter 5 Opened
publicClues.text: Fear Garden continues at Mid City Beer Garden. Chapter 5: The Root Ledger is active. This chapter is built for Senior Detective.

publicClues.title: Root Ledger Thread
publicClues.text: The shell-company buyer, fake vendor initials, and staged digital records all lead back to one person with access to guest check-in, manager credentials, and expansion paperwork. Watch for evidence that connects this chapter to the larger Fear Garden saga.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Andre Moss missed two calls from Lena Vale before the garden music cut out. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Andre moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Andre texted Lena: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.” This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Lena replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Andre’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Lena’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the beer garden expansion. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Lena. It also brushes against the Root Ledger thread running through the full Fear Garden story.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Lena near the restricted service path before the incident. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: duplicate badge moves while Trevor is visible outside.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Andre disappears from frame. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Lena saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Andre saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Andre. This clue belongs to Chapter 5: The Root Ledger.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident. It also brushes against the Root Ledger thread running through the full Fear Garden story.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata connects the edit to the suspect’s access trail: Lena Vale. It also brushes against the Root Ledger thread running through the full Fear Garden story.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome back to Fear Garden at Mid City Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This is Chapter 5: The Root Ledger, built for Senior Detective. Fear Garden is no longer a set of separate cases. It is one continuing story that grows darker and more elaborate with every chapter. The shell-company buyer, fake vendor initials, and staged digital records all lead back to one person with access to guest check-in, manager credentials, and expansion paperwork. Tonight's investigation focuses on The Beer Garden Truth. The senior chapter is the full conspiracy solve. Players must reinterpret earlier evidence, spot the staged frame job, and identify the person who used everyone else’s crimes as cover. Study every app carefully. Earlier details may return with new meaning, and the Root Ledger thread is now part of the case foundation.
revealScript: Case revealed. Chapter 5: The Root Ledger is closed. The culprit was Lena Vale. The method: Used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame Trevor. The motive: To cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the Beer Garden expansion. The proof: Duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving trevor’s login was staged. Lena Vale did not commit every crime in the garden, but she built the system that made every crime useful. Fear Garden closes with the Root Ledger exposed—and one outside name still blacked out.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Andre’s death suspicious?

options.label: A normal spilled pint near the stage
duplicate badge moves while Trevor is visible outside
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

previousChapterConnection: The shell-company buyer, fake vendor initials, and staged digital records all lead back to one person with access to guest check-in, manager credentials, and expansion paperwork.
